metadata.title: Cosmic Atlas Structure Catalog
metadata.note: Generated from the user-provided cosmic_atlas_structure_catalog workbook.

milkyWay.Name: Galactic Center
milkyWay.Location / use note: The dynamical and photometric center of the Milky Way.

milkyWay.Name: Sagittarius A*
milkyWay.Location / use note: Central black hole of the Milky Way.

milkyWay.Name: Milky Way Nuclear Star Cluster
milkyWay.Location / use note: Dense cluster surrounding Sagittarius A*.

milkyWay.Name: Central Molecular Zone
milkyWay.Location / use note: Dense molecular gas and active star formation around the Galactic Center.

milkyWay.Name: Galactic Bulge
milkyWay.Location / use note: Dense central concentration of mostly older stars.

milkyWay.Name: Milky Way Bar
milkyWay.Location / use note: Elongated stellar structure through the center.

milkyWay.Name: Thin Disk
milkyWay.Location / use note: Flattened disk containing most young stars, gas, and dust.

milkyWay.Name: Thick Disk
milkyWay.Location / use note: Older, vertically extended stellar population.

milkyWay.Name: Stellar Halo
milkyWay.Location / use note: Diffuse halo containing old stars and globular clusters.

milkyWay.Name: Dark Matter Halo
milkyWay.Location / use note: Inferred extended mass halo; not directly luminous.

milkyWay.Name: Gaseous Disk
milkyWay.Location / use note: Hydrogen-rich disk containing star-forming material.

milkyWay.Name: Galactic Warp
milkyWay.Location / use note: The outer disk bends above and below the midplane.

milkyWay.Name: Galactic Flare
milkyWay.Location / use note: The gas and stellar disk becomes vertically thicker with radius.

milkyWay.Name: Norma Arm
milkyWay.Location / use note: One of the Milky Way's major inner spiral arms.

milkyWay.Name: Scutum–Centaurus Arm
milkyWay.Location / use note: Major arm passing through the inner Galaxy and toward Centaurus.

milkyWay.Name: Sagittarius–Carina Arm
milkyWay.Location / use note: Prominent star-forming arm containing many bright nebulae.

milkyWay.Name: Perseus Arm
milkyWay.Location / use note: Major outer spiral arm visible through many star-forming regions.

milkyWay.Name: Outer Arm
milkyWay.Location / use note: Distant arm outside the Perseus Arm.

milkyWay.Name: Orion Spur
milkyWay.Location / use note: Minor spiral feature containing the Sun and many nearby star-forming clouds.

milkyWay.Name: Local Bubble
milkyWay.Location / use note: A cavity in the interstellar medium extending hundreds of light-years.

milkyWay.Name: Gould Belt
milkyWay.Location / use note: Inclined system of nearby young stars and molecular clouds.

milkyWay.Name: Radcliffe Wave
milkyWay.Location / use note: Large undulating chain of nearby stellar nurseries.

milkyWay.Name: Fermi Bubbles
milkyWay.Location / use note: Two giant gamma-ray emitting lobes extending from the center.

milkyWay.Name: eROSITA Bubbles
milkyWay.Location / use note: Large X-ray structures broadly aligned with the Fermi bubbles.

milkyWay.Name: North Polar Spur
milkyWay.Location / use note: Large radio and X-ray arc; distance and origin remain debated.

milkyWay.Name: Magellanic Stream
milkyWay.Location / use note: Long stream of gas associated with the Magellanic Clouds.

milkyWay.Name: Leading Arm
milkyWay.Location / use note: Gas extending ahead of the Magellanic Clouds' motion.

milkyWay.Name: Smith Cloud
milkyWay.Location / use note: Massive high-velocity H I cloud moving toward the Galactic disk.

milkyWay.Name: Complex C
milkyWay.Location / use note: Large metal-poor H I complex in the halo.

milkyWay.Name: Monoceros Ring
milkyWay.Location / use note: Ring-like stellar overdensity around the outer Galaxy.

milkyWay.Name: Solar System
milkyWay.Location / use note: The Sun, eight planets, dwarf planets, small bodies, and heliosphere.

milkyWay.Name: Alpha Centauri System
milkyWay.Location / use note: Nearest stellar system to the Sun; includes Proxima Centauri.

milkyWay.Name: Proxima Centauri System
milkyWay.Location / use note: Nearest known exoplanet-hosting star.

milkyWay.Name: Barnard's Star System
milkyWay.Location / use note: Nearby high-proper-motion red dwarf with reported planet candidates.

milkyWay.Name: Sirius System
milkyWay.Location / use note: Bright A-type star with a white-dwarf companion.

milkyWay.Name: Epsilon Eridani System
milkyWay.Location / use note: Nearby star with a debris disk and giant planet.

milkyWay.Name: Tau Ceti System
milkyWay.Location / use note: Nearby Sun-like star with debris and planet candidates.

milkyWay.Name: TRAPPIST-1 System
milkyWay.Location / use note: Seven known roughly Earth-sized planets.

milkyWay.Name: LHS 1140 System
milkyWay.Location / use note: Nearby system with a transiting super-Earth.

milkyWay.Name: 55 Cancri System
milkyWay.Location / use note: Bright nearby star with five known planets.

milkyWay.Name: HD 189733 System
milkyWay.Location / use note: Nearby transiting exoplanet system.

milkyWay.Name: Beta Pictoris System
milkyWay.Location / use note: Young system with directly imaged giant planets.

milkyWay.Name: HR 8799 System
milkyWay.Location / use note: Four directly imaged giant planets orbit a young star.

milkyWay.Name: PDS 70 System
milkyWay.Location / use note: Young star with a disk and directly imaged forming planets.

milkyWay.Name: Kepler-90 System
milkyWay.Location / use note: Known system with eight planets.

milkyWay.Name: Orion Molecular Cloud Complex
milkyWay.Location / use note: Includes Orion A, Orion B, the Orion Nebula, and nearby dark clouds.

milkyWay.Name: Orion Nebula (M42)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Bright star-forming region in Orion A.

milkyWay.Name: Horsehead Nebula (Barnard 33)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Dense dust cloud silhouetted against IC 434.

milkyWay.Name: Flame Nebula (NGC 2024)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Active star-forming region near Alnitak.

milkyWay.Name: Barnard's Loop
milkyWay.Location / use note: Large arc of ionized gas around the Orion complex.

milkyWay.Name: Taurus Molecular Cloud
milkyWay.Location / use note: One of the nearest low-mass star-forming regions.

milkyWay.Name: Perseus Molecular Cloud
milkyWay.Location / use note: Contains NGC 1333 and IC 348.

milkyWay.Name: NGC 1333
milkyWay.Location / use note: Young stellar nursery in the Perseus cloud.

milkyWay.Name: Ophiuchus Molecular Cloud
milkyWay.Location / use note: Nearby cloud forming low-mass stars.

milkyWay.Name: Rho Ophiuchi Cloud Complex
milkyWay.Location / use note: Colorful nearby region of gas, dust, and young stars.

milkyWay.Name: Pipe Nebula
milkyWay.Location / use note: Large dark molecular cloud projected toward the Galactic center.

milkyWay.Name: Chamaeleon I Cloud
milkyWay.Location / use note: Nearby region of low-mass star formation.

milkyWay.Name: Coalsack Nebula
milkyWay.Location / use note: Prominent dark cloud near the Southern Cross.

milkyWay.Name: Gum Nebula
milkyWay.Location / use note: Very large ionized shell in the southern sky.

milkyWay.Name: Vela Molecular Ridge
milkyWay.Location / use note: Massive star-forming clouds along the Vela region.

milkyWay.Name: Rosette Nebula
milkyWay.Location / use note: Large circular emission nebula surrounding NGC 2244.

milkyWay.Name: Cone Nebula
milkyWay.Location / use note: Dark pillar within the NGC 2264 complex.

milkyWay.Name: California Nebula (NGC 1499)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Extended emission nebula ionized by Xi Persei.

milkyWay.Name: Heart Nebula (IC 1805)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Large star-forming complex with embedded clusters.

milkyWay.Name: Soul Nebula (IC 1848)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Companion star-forming region near the Heart Nebula.

milkyWay.Name: North America Nebula (NGC 7000)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Large emission nebula near Deneb.

milkyWay.Name: Pelican Nebula (IC 5070)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Star-forming region adjacent to the North America Nebula.

milkyWay.Name: Cygnus X
milkyWay.Location / use note: One of the richest nearby regions of massive star formation.

milkyWay.Name: Cocoon Nebula (IC 5146)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Young cluster and glowing cloud at the end of Barnard 168.

milkyWay.Name: Eagle Nebula (M16)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Contains the Pillars of Creation and cluster NGC 6611.

milkyWay.Name: Omega Nebula (M17)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Massive star-forming region also called the Swan Nebula.

milkyWay.Name: Lagoon Nebula (M8)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Bright emission nebula with active star formation.

milkyWay.Name: Trifid Nebula (M20)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Nebula divided by dark dust lanes.

milkyWay.Name: Carina Nebula (NGC 3372)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Massive stellar nursery containing Eta Carinae and Trumpler clusters.

milkyWay.Name: Running Chicken Nebula (IC 2602 region/IC 2944)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Star-forming region containing Bok globules.

milkyWay.Name: Cat's Paw Nebula (NGC 6334)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Massive star-forming complex with embedded clusters.

milkyWay.Name: Lobster Nebula (NGC 6357)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Massive star-forming region containing Pismis 24.

milkyWay.Name: RCW 49
milkyWay.Location / use note: Ionized region surrounding Westerlund 2.

milkyWay.Name: W49A
milkyWay.Location / use note: One of the Milky Way's most luminous star-forming regions.

milkyWay.Name: W51
milkyWay.Location / use note: Large high-mass star-forming complex.

milkyWay.Name: Sagittarius B2
milkyWay.Location / use note: Extremely massive, chemically rich cloud near the Galactic Center.

milkyWay.Name: Sagittarius C
milkyWay.Location / use note: Star-forming region roughly 200 light-years in projection from Sagittarius A*.

milkyWay.Name: Pleiades (M45)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Young nearby open cluster.

milkyWay.Name: Hyades
milkyWay.Location / use note: Nearest major open cluster.

milkyWay.Name: Praesepe (M44)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Nearby open cluster also called the Beehive.

milkyWay.Name: Alpha Persei Cluster
milkyWay.Location / use note: Young moving group and open cluster.

milkyWay.Name: Coma Star Cluster
milkyWay.Location / use note: Sparse nearby open cluster.

milkyWay.Name: IC 2391
milkyWay.Location / use note: Young southern open cluster.

milkyWay.Name: IC 2602
milkyWay.Location / use note: Southern Pleiades open cluster.

milkyWay.Name: NGC 2516
milkyWay.Location / use note: Rich young open cluster.

milkyWay.Name: NGC 2244
milkyWay.Location / use note: Young cluster inside the Rosette Nebula.

milkyWay.Name: NGC 2264
milkyWay.Location / use note: Cluster associated with the Cone Nebula.

milkyWay.Name: NGC 6611
milkyWay.Location / use note: Young cluster powering the Eagle Nebula.

milkyWay.Name: NGC 6530
milkyWay.Location / use note: Young cluster in the Lagoon Nebula.

milkyWay.Name: Westerlund 1
milkyWay.Location / use note: One of the most massive young clusters in the Milky Way.

milkyWay.Name: Westerlund 2
milkyWay.Location / use note: Massive cluster within RCW 49.

milkyWay.Name: Arches Cluster
milkyWay.Location / use note: Very dense young cluster near Sagittarius A*.

milkyWay.Name: Quintuplet Cluster
milkyWay.Location / use note: Massive cluster near the Galactic Center.

milkyWay.Name: Double Cluster (NGC 869 and NGC 884)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Two young neighboring clusters.

milkyWay.Name: Omega Centauri (NGC 5139)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Largest and most massive Milky Way globular cluster.

milkyWay.Name: 47 Tucanae (NGC 104)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Massive bright southern globular cluster.

milkyWay.Name: M4 (NGC 6121)
milkyWay.Location / use note: One of the nearest globular clusters.

milkyWay.Name: M13 (NGC 6205)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Prominent northern globular cluster.

milkyWay.Name: M15 (NGC 7078)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Dense core-collapsed globular cluster.

milkyWay.Name: M22 (NGC 6656)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Bright nearby globular cluster.

milkyWay.Name: M5 (NGC 5904)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Large old globular cluster.

milkyWay.Name: NGC 6397
milkyWay.Location / use note: One of the nearest globular clusters.

milkyWay.Name: Crab Nebula (M1)
milkyWay.Location / use note: Remnant of the supernova observed in 1054; contains the Crab Pulsar.

milkyWay.Name: Crab Pulsar
milkyWay.Location / use note: Rapidly rotating neutron star inside the Crab Nebula.

milkyWay.Name: Cassiopeia A
milkyWay.Location / use note: Young remnant from a stellar explosion roughly 350 years ago.

milkyWay.Name: Vela Supernova Remnant
milkyWay.Location / use note: Large nearby supernova remnant.

milkyWay.Name: Vela Pulsar
milkyWay.Location / use note: Pulsar associated with the Vela remnant.

milkyWay.Name: Cygnus Loop
milkyWay.Location / use note: Large nearby supernova remnant also called the Veil Nebula complex.

milkyWay.Name: Veil Nebula
milkyWay.Location / use note: Visible filamentary portion of the Cygnus Loop.

milkyWay.Name: Puppis A
milkyWay.Location / use note: Young oxygen-rich supernova remnant.

milkyWay.Name: SN 1006 Remnant
milkyWay.Location / use note: Remnant of the bright supernova observed in 1006.

milkyWay.Name: Tycho's Supernova Remnant
milkyWay.Location / use note: Remnant of SN 1572.

milkyWay.Name: Kepler's Supernova Remnant
milkyWay.Location / use note: Remnant of SN 1604.

milkyWay.Name: G1.9+0.3
milkyWay.Location / use note: Youngest known supernova remnant in the Milky Way.

milkyWay.Name: W49B
milkyWay.Location / use note: Bright X-ray and radio remnant.

milkyWay.Name: RX J1713.7−3946
milkyWay.Location / use note: TeV gamma-ray bright remnant.

milkyWay.Name: W50 / Manatee Nebula
milkyWay.Location / use note: Large radio nebula shaped by SS 433's jets.

milkyWay.Name: SS 433
milkyWay.Location / use note: Accreting compact object launching precessing relativistic jets.

milkyWay.Name: Cygnus X-1
milkyWay.Location / use note: One of the best-established stellar-mass black holes.

milkyWay.Name: V404 Cygni
milkyWay.Location / use note: Transient black-hole binary.

milkyWay.Name: GRS 1915+105
milkyWay.Location / use note: Accreting black hole with relativistic jets.

milkyWay.Name: Gaia BH1
milkyWay.Location / use note: Nearby dormant black hole identified through stellar motion.

milkyWay.Name: Gaia BH2
milkyWay.Location / use note: Dormant black hole in a wide binary.

milkyWay.Name: PSR B1919+21
milkyWay.Location / use note: First pulsar discovered.

milkyWay.Name: PSR J0437−4715
milkyWay.Location / use note: One of the nearest and brightest millisecond pulsars.

milkyWay.Name: SGR 1806−20
milkyWay.Location / use note: Highly magnetized neutron star that produced a giant flare.

milkyWay.Name: XTE J1810−197
milkyWay.Location / use note: First magnetar detected as a transient radio pulsar.

groupsAndClusters.Name: Local Group
groupsAndClusters.Key members: Milky Way, M31, M33, Magellanic Clouds

groupsAndClusters.Name: M81 Group
groupsAndClusters.Relation to Milky Way: Nearest prominent interacting group beyond the Local Group

groupsAndClusters.Name: Centaurus A Group
groupsAndClusters.Relation to Milky Way: Nearby group dominated by Centaurus A

groupsAndClusters.Name: M83 Group
groupsAndClusters.Relation to Milky Way: Neighboring group often paired with the Centaurus A Group

groupsAndClusters.Name: IC 342/Maffei Group
groupsAndClusters.Relation to Milky Way: Nearby group obscured by the Milky Way

groupsAndClusters.Name: M101 Group
groupsAndClusters.Relation to Milky Way: Loose group centered on the Pinwheel Galaxy

groupsAndClusters.Name: Leo I Group
groupsAndClusters.Relation to Milky Way: Nearby group containing the Leo I galaxies

groupsAndClusters.Name: Eridanus Group
groupsAndClusters.Relation to Milky Way: Large nearby aggregation between groups and cluster scale

groupsAndClusters.Name: Norma Cluster (Abell 3627)
groupsAndClusters.Relation to Milky Way: Massive cluster near the Great Attractor

groupsAndClusters.Name: Local Void
groupsAndClusters.Approx. distance / span (million ly): Adjacent, tens of millions of ly
groupsAndClusters.Key members: Low-density region next to the Local Sheet

groupsAndClusters.Name: Virgo Supercluster
groupsAndClusters.Key members: Local Group, Virgo Cluster, many groups
groupsAndClusters.Relation to Milky Way: Traditional large-scale structure containing the Local Group

groupsAndClusters.Name: Laniakea Supercluster
groupsAndClusters.Key members: Virgo region, Great Attractor and connected flows
groupsAndClusters.Relation to Milky Way: Large gravitational basin containing the Milky Way
